findings.title: Hard-coded secret or token
findings.rationale: Leaked credentials in source code can directly compromise payment and user data systems.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to a vault or environment variable and rotate the exposed value.

findings.title: Hard-coded secret or token
findings.rationale: Leaked credentials in source code can directly compromise payment and user data systems.
findings.recommendation: Move secrets to a vault or environment variable and rotate the exposed value.

findings.title: Potential SQL injection
findings.evidence: return cursor.execute(f"select * from payments where user_id = '{user_id}'")
findings.rationale: Payment and settlement queries must not concatenate user-controlled input.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized SQL or repository helpers that bind values.

findings.title: Sensitive personal or payment data in logs
findings.rationale: Logs are copied to observability and incident systems; raw PII creates privacy and compliance risk.
findings.recommendation: Mask or hash sensitive fields before logging and define retention rules.
findings.fix_example: logger.info('payment approved user=%s card=%s', user_id, mask_card(card_no))

findings.title: No automated tests detected
findings.evidence: source files exist but no test files were found
findings.rationale: Payment changes need executable evidence for rollback-safe releases.
findings.recommendation: Add focused unit tests and at least one integration test around payment success, failure, duplicate request, and timeout cases.
findings.fix_example: tests/test_payment_service.py with duplicate payment and timeout scenarios

findings.title: README is missing
findings.rationale: Financial services need operational context, ownership, and runbook links during incidents.
findings.recommendation: Add README sections for owner, APIs, dependencies, SLO, rollback, and FDS/security notes.
findings.fix_example: README.md: owner, endpoints, alert channels, rollback command, known risk controls

fix_plan.change: Move secrets to a vault or environment variable and rotate the exposed value.
Use parameterized SQL or repository helpers that bind values.
Mask or hash sensitive fields before logging and define retention rules.
fix_plan.suggested_code: logger.info('payment approved user=%s card=%s', user_id, mask_card(card_no))
